---
title: "complex_recompose"
generated: true
description: "Reconstitute a complex-valued tensor from real-valued tensors denoting its amplitude and its phase."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.numerics.tensor_ops`](../nitrix-numerics-tensor_ops.qmd)

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix._internal.util`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:complex_recompose -->
```python
complex_recompose(ampl: Float[Array, ...], phase: Float[Array, ...]) -> Complex[Array, ...]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:complex_recompose -->
Reconstitute a complex-valued tensor from real-valued tensors denoting its
amplitude and its phase.

Together with
[complex_decompose](nitrix._internal.util.complex_decompose.html), this
function provides a uniform interface for switching between complex and
polar representations of complex numbers. This enables us to change the
implementation of these operations in one place when we find the most
efficient and stable method.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:complex_recompose -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `ampl` | `Float[Array, ...]` | — | — | Real-valued array storing complex number amplitudes. |
| `phase` | `Float[Array, ...]` | — | — | Real-valued array storing complex number phases in radians. |

**Returns** `Complex[Array, ...]`. Complex numbers formed from the specified amplitudes and phases.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:complex_recompose -->
### See also

[`complex_decompose`](../nitrix-numerics-tensor_ops/complex_decompose.qmd)
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
